name: RAG Poisoning
description: Corrupt a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline by injecting malicious documents into the vector database. Explore how poisoned context shapes model outputs and enables covert instruction injection through seemingly legitimate documents.
author: DVAP Research Team
objectives: Inject a malicious document into the vector store
Cause the document to surface in retrieval for target queries
Use retrieved content to hijack model instructions
Manipulate factual responses through poisoned context
Demonstrate ranking manipulation via embedding similarity
attack_surface: Retrieval pipeline that treats all chunks as trusted
No content moderation on indexed documents
Embedding model susceptible to adversarial perturbations
mitigations: Document source authentication and provenance tracking
Retrieval result sandboxing in model context
detection_opportunities: Monitor retrieval patterns for anomalous document surfacing
Semantic outlier detection in retrieved chunks

architecture.data_flow: Query -> Embedding -> Qdrant search -> Top-K chunks -> Prompt assembly -> LLM
trust_boundaries: Retrieved chunks are injected as context

threat_actors: External attacker with document upload access
threat_model.impact: Disinformation, instruction injection, data exfiltration through model output

challenges.name: Poison the Well
challenges.description: Upload a document that gets retrieved when users ask about company security policies, containing injected instructions.
hints: Documents are chunked and embedded as-is
Craft your document to be semantically close to policy-related queries

challenges.name: Rewrite History
challenges.description: Replace a legitimate fact in the knowledge base with your own version by exploiting the ranking algorithm.
hints: Higher cosine similarity wins retrieval ranking
Adversarial perturbations can push your document above legitimate ones
